title: Enable Direct URL Proxy Feature

requirements.text: Implement a backend `proxyUrl` function in the Motoko actor that accepts a URL string, performs an HTTP outcall to fetch the resource, and returns the response body (HTML/content) along with relevant headers (content-type, status).
acceptanceCriteria: A `proxyUrl(url: Text)` public function exists in the backend actor.
The function performs an outbound HTTP GET request to the given URL.
The function returns the response body and HTTP status code.
A `transform` callback or equivalent is provided for response sanitization.

requirements.text: Add a `proxyUrl` React Query mutation in `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` that calls the backend `proxyUrl` actor method, parses the response, and returns the proxied content for rendering.
acceptanceCriteria: A `useProxyUrl` mutation (or equivalent) is defined in `useQueries.ts`.
It calls the backend actor's `proxyUrl` method with the given URL.
On success, it returns the proxied HTML content.
On error, it surfaces an error message usable by the UI.

requirements.text: Wire the direct URL proxy mutation into `BrowserLayout.tsx` so that when the user enters a URL (rather than a search query) in the address bar, the app calls `proxyUrl` and renders the returned content inside `ProxyContent`.
acceptanceCriteria: Entering a valid URL in the address bar and submitting triggers the proxy mutation, not a search.
The proxied HTML is passed to `ProxyContent` for iframe rendering.
Loading and error states are reflected in the tab bar and UI.
The address bar URL is updated to reflect the navigated URL after successful proxy.

constraints: All backend logic must remain in the single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo.
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Adding support for non-HTTP protocols (e.g., FTP, WebSocket).
Caching proxied content on the backend.
Authentication or login-gating for the proxy feature.
Modifying the Google Custom Search integration.
